element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Available from start; basic offensive capability
Powered up via green power-ups; expands tactical options
Blue power-ups regenerate/improve shield; critical survival mechanic
Orange power-ups grant additional ships; extends game length
Emergency relief tool against overwhelming enemy formations
Switches between Darius and Darius II shot patterns; strategic variety

typical_relief_triggers: Collecting weapon/shield power-ups from square-shaped enemies
typical_tension_triggers: Enemies approaching from front and behind simultaneously
Loss of shield protection (blue power-up depletion)
Power-up loss upon death (though collected items persist post-destruction)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Constant horizontal scroll with escalating enemy pressure; difficulty noted as high; power-up dependency creates cycles of vulnerability and resilience

difficulty_curve.early_game: Baseline threat; player accumulates initial power-ups and learns enemy patterns
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating enemy density and variety; power-up management becomes critical
difficulty_curve.late_game: High sustained pressure; multiple difficulty settings available (Easy/Normal)
notable_spikes: Boss encounters requiring sustained weapon fire and shield management
Sections combining front and rear enemy approach vectors

notable_level_patterns.description: Square-shaped enemies carry weapon, shield, and special power-ups; defeating them is primary resource acquisition mechanic. Death does not reset collected power-ups, encouraging aggressive play despite risk
Enemies approach from both front and behind player simultaneously, forcing constant spatial awareness and defensive positioning
Two points in campaign present red power-ups allowing strategic switching between Darius (original) and Darius II (alternate) shot mechanics, adding replayability and tactical depth
